message:
fix(mysql): use negotiated capabilities for result-set parsing and harden protocol handling

Three related MySQL protocol correctness fixes:

1. Capability negotiation: Store the intersection of server and client
   capabilities (server_caps & client_caps) rather than the raw server
   capabilities. The previous code set self.inner.capabilities to the
   server's advertised superset during handshake, then overwrote it
   during authentication — but packet parsing decisions between those
   two points could use the wrong capability set. Now
   negotiated_capabilities() is called once during auth and the result
   is stored before any response packets are read.

2. Column-terminator parsing: read_result_set() previously skipped
   reading the column-terminator packet entirely when CLIENT_DEPRECATE_EOF
   was set. The MySQL protocol still sends a terminator (an OK packet
   instead of EOF) — the flag only changes its format. Now both paths
   read the packet and validate its type (is_eof_packet vs
   is_result_set_ok_packet) with proper length/structure checks.

3. Trailing-byte detection: parse_text_row() now returns an error if
   the packet has unconsumed bytes after all column values are parsed,
   catching malformed or misframed row packets.

Added tests for capability negotiation (verifying intersection logic)
and trailing-byte rejection in row parsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
